title: Burns

steps.title: Stop the burning process
steps.instruction: Remove the person from the heat source. Remove any clothing or jewelry near the burn — but do NOT remove anything stuck to the skin.

steps.title: Cool the burn
steps.instruction: Cool the burn immediately with clean, cool running water for at least 20 minutes. Do NOT use ice, ice water, butter, or oils — they make it worse.

steps.title: Check severity
steps.instruction: Is the burn larger than your palm? Does it affect the face, hands, feet, joints, or genitals? Are there blisters, white, brown, or black skin? If yes to any — call emergency services immediately.

steps.title: Call for help if needed
steps.instruction: Call emergency services (101) if the burn is severe, covers a large area, involves a child or elderly person, or is a chemical or electrical burn.

steps.title: Cover the burn
steps.instruction: Leave the burn uncovered if possible. If you must cover it, use a loose, clean, non-adhesive dressing or plastic wrap. Never use adhesive bandages — they stick to the skin.

steps.title: Manage pain
steps.instruction: Give an over-the-counter pain reliever such as acetaminophen or ibuprofen. Apply aloe vera lotion for minor burns only. Encourage the person to drink fluids.

steps.title: Monitor the person
steps.instruction: Watch for signs of infection: redness spreading, swelling, pus, or fever. Do NOT break blisters — they protect the skin. Seek medical care if pain persists or condition worsens.

warnings: Do NOT use ice or ice water
Do NOT apply butter, oils, or ointments
sources: American Red Cross First Aid Guidelines
